items.title: 'I Don't Have Hundreds Of Thousands Of Dollars,' Says Elon Musk's 21-Year-Old Daughter Who Lives With Roommates To Save On Rent
items.description: Vivian Wilson, daughter of Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, said she lives with three roommates in Los Angeles because it is "cheaper" and she often worries about money. "People assume I have a lot of money. I don't have hundreds of thousands of dollars at my disposal," the 21-year-old told New York magazine recently. Despite being the oldest of Musk's 14 children, Wilson said she does not benefit financially from his fortune and is working to build her own path. Don't Miss: Kevin O'Leary

items.title: Sapia.ai Appoints Kim Macdonald as Head of Growth
items.description: SYDNEY, September 24, 2025--Sapia.ai appoints Kim Macdonald as Head of Growth to drive enterprise expansion.

items.title: AI Arms Race Intensifies Again. Here Are the 2 Big Takeaways For Investors.
items.description: A slowdown in the race to invest in and build AI infrastructure isn’t in sight yet, which bodes well for the AI trade.

items.title: Amazon Stock Upgraded To Buy By Analyst. Why Its AI Push Shouldn't Be Overlooked.
items.description: Amazon stock appears to be stuck in the mud but this analyst expects its Anthropic partnership will soon start to pay off.

items.title: Fmr. OpenAI Exec: Competitive Landscape Moving Quickly
items.description: Microsoft Corp. will start using artificial intelligence models from Anthropic to help power its workplace AI assistant, adding a significant partner to a product that has so far been predominantly driven by OpenAI. This comes as Nvidia gets set to invest as much as $100 billion in OpenAI to help the ChatGPT maker support a massive build-out of data centers equipped with Nvidia's chips. Former OpenAI Global Affairs Head of Strategic Partnerships Lane Dilg joined Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec on 'Bloomberg Businessweek Live' at the Bloomberg Philanthropies Global Forum to discuss global artificial intelligence competition.
